title: cerasus undulata
intro: Cerasus undulata, also known as the wild cherry or mountain cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains a compound called amygdalin, a natural source of benzaldehyde and prunasin. The medicinal properties of Cerasus undulata include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote oral health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues.
